mechanism_cases.expected: KEEP_LAST retains and replays exactly the newest depth samples in sequence order
KEEP_ALL accepts through capacity and rejects the next sample without eviction
duplicate sequence numbers reach the application at most once
gaps and repair requests never read outside retained history
a participant reset does not reuse or deliver stale pre-reset history
acknowledged or evicted slots release pbuf ownership immediately and heap recovers
a late Volatile reader receives zero pre-join samples
a late Transient Local reader receives each eligible cached sample once in order
replay contains no more than the configured depth and contains the newest eligible samples
restarted writers never replay samples from the previous participant epoch
fragmented replay either passes exact reconstruction or remains explicitly unsupported and excluded
an infinite Deadline emits no miss event
no miss is emitted before the configured boundary
the frozen timer-resolution rule emits the first miss at the specified boundary
multiple elapsed periods increment the exact missed-period count without duplicate callbacks
a timely sample resets the next deadline and later misses use the new baseline
recorded event latency is bounded by deadline plus the frozen polling tolerance
an infinite Lifespan never expires a sample
a sample younger than Lifespan is transmitted exactly once
a sample older than Lifespan is not transmitted and increments the expiry counter once
the exact equality boundary follows the preregistered timer-resolution rule
expired history is not delivered after reader reconnect
board release-hold start and end timestamps reconcile with the injected delay
no false loss occurs before match and the first valid heartbeat
no loss occurs before the lease boundary
crossing the lease emits one lost transition
repeated checks during one outage do not emit duplicate lost transitions
the first valid post-outage heartbeat emits one recovered transition
an infinite lease emits neither lost nor recovered transitions
exactly max_samples outstanding changes are accepted
the next outstanding sample is rejected and increments the reject counter once
max_bytes accepts the exact boundary and rejects the first excess byte
KEEP_ALL rejects at the limit while KEEP_LAST eviction remains separately defined
allocation failure is counted and never reported as successful publication
publishing resumes after acknowledgments free history
pressure and rejection preserve cache invariants and stack guards
